user_query:
Act as the Product Owner for our SaaS, TaskFlow. Please read the following inputs and deliver a groomed backlog, prioritization summary, and a sprint plan.

Read these files under input/:
- input/product_context.md
- input/raw_backlog.csv
- input/stakeholder_requests.json
- input/team_capacity.json

Based on those, do the following and write the results to the specified paths under output/:
1) Backlog grooming (output/backlog/taskflow.md)
- Structure into three sections with headings exactly: "Ready for Sprint", "Needs Refinement", and "Parking Lot".
- Convert backlog items into user stories in the format: As a [user], I want [capability], So that [benefit].
- For every story in "Ready for Sprint":
  - Include at least two acceptance criteria using Given/When/Then lines (Gherkin style) that are independently testable.
  - Add an Estimate: [number] line and a Priority: [Must|Should|Could|Won't] line (use MoSCoW).
- Apply the 20/30/50 grooming rule (top ~20% fully ready, next ~30% needs refinement, remaining ~50% in parking lot). Make the most time-critical and risk-reducing work Ready.
- If any raw backlog item has effort_points > 8, split it into smaller stories (reflect the split in the backlog with distinct story titles and estimates) and do not put the entire large item in Ready as a single story.
- Ensure the "Confetti animation" idea is clearly marked Won't for this sprint or placed in Parking Lot.

2) Sprint plan for Sprint 07 (output/sprints/sprint-07.md)
- Include sections titled exactly: "Sprint Goal", "Capacity", "Committed Stories", "Definition of Done", and "Out of Scope".
- Set Capacity to match input/team_capacity.json and include a line in the file that reads exactly: Capacity: 20.
- Commit to these Must-have stories this sprint (they must appear in Committed Stories): "GDPR consent banner", "Email verification", and "Fix SSO logout bug".
- Keep total committed Estimate points less than or equal to Capacity. Include a line that reads exactly: Total Points: [sum].
- For each committed story, include its story text, at least two Given/When/Then acceptance criteria, an Estimate: [number], and Priority: [MoSCoW category].
- Copy the Definition of Done from input/product_context.md (verbatim or summarized) into the "Definition of Done" section.
- In "Out of Scope", list at least one Won't item for this sprint (e.g., the confetti animation delighter).

3) Prioritization summary (output/prioritization.csv)
- Create a CSV with header: id,title,priority,estimate,type
- Include a row for every story in the "Ready for Sprint" section of output/backlog/taskflow.md.
- Ensure rows for the following titles exist and have priority Must with numeric estimates: "GDPR consent banner", "Email verification", and "Fix SSO logout bug".

General rules:
- Use MoSCoW categorization consistently across backlog and sprint plan.
- Ensure all acceptance criteria use the words Given, When, Then in that order on separate lines.
- Ensure the sprint Total Points does not exceed Capacity.
- Keep language clear, testable, and aligned with INVEST.

Deliverables to write:
- output/backlog/taskflow.md
- output/sprints/sprint-07.md
- output/prioritization.csv
